title: Easytrieve with Code4z: Generate, Validate, and Execute

steps.title: Prerequisites and Code4z Extensions
hints: You need three extensions named in the source material: Easytrieve Language Support, JCL Language Support, and Zowe Explorer.
A Zowe profile is what connects VS Code to your mainframe session — list it explicitly in your notes file.

steps.title: Hello World Easytrieve Program
hints: The sample program uses DEFINE for a varying work field, JOB INPUT NULL, DISPLAY, then STOP.
Easytrieve keywords are not case-sensitive, but keep DISPLAY text in quotes as shown.

steps.title: Syntax, Symbols, and Navigation
hints: Use an asterisk in column 1 for comment lines the assembler-style way, or leading * after optional blanks per your shop standard — the validator accepts * in the first non-space position.
One comment line per IDE feature keeps the file readable and passes the check.

steps.title: JCL to Execute Easytrieve
hints: The executor program for Easytrieve is EZTPA00 — your EXEC statement must reference it.
SYSIN must contain the same Easytrieve source statements you run from the .ezt file, ending before the next JCL statement.

steps.title: Upload Targets and Submitting the Job
hints: Extension settings use eztdsn for the Easytrieve source library and jcldsn for the JCL library — use those exact names in lowercase in your file.
Mention both upload and submit paths: right-click the Easytrieve source for upload, then submit via Zowe Explorer or the extension on the JCL member.

steps.title: Jobs, Spool, and Completion Codes
hints: A normal completion is often shown as 0000 in messages and listings.
Zowe Explorer groups submitted work under Jobs; DDs such as SYSPRINT hold listing output.
